expectations.text: Runs 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py write <file> Inp_Price 125' (or equivalent path)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 shows: 'Command: uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py write ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Price 125'. Output: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Price", "value": 125, "cell": "Assumptions!B2"}.

expectations.text: Runs 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py read <file> Inp_Price' to verify the write
expectations.evidence: Step 3 shows: 'Command: uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py read ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Price'. Output: {"status": "ok", "values": {"Inp_Price": 125}}.

expectations.text: The read command returns the updated value 125 (not the original 100)
Both commands produce JSON output with status 'ok'
expectations.evidence: Write output: {"status": "ok",...}. Read output: {"status": "ok",...}. Both show status 'ok'.

expectations.text: Does not modify the file manually via openpyxl or other means — uses the idfa_ops.py script exclusively
expectations.evidence: Tool Used: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py from skills/idfa-ops/'. All operations use the idfa_ops.py script. No mention of direct openpyxl usage.
